behavior_summary: U8P_WTABLE::look: When the table object is examined (Event 0) it sets item-level bookkeeping (an info key and process-exclude state), constructs the literal string "table ", and invokes Item::bark to present that string to the requesting process/player. After the bark it performs the VM/handler cleanup (stack/implies/pop) and returns the normal U8P_WTABLE::look-handler result.

U8P_WTABLE::gotHit: On hit (Event 6) the table sets item bookkeeping (info and process-exclude), inspects the second event parameter and, if that parameter matches a specific sentinel condition (inferred to be -1 / 0xFFFF), calls Item::setFrame to change the table's displayed frame to 1 — i.e., modify appearance after a particular hit condition.
notable_control_flow: Look handler flow: initialize frame/info bookkeeping -> push/construct literal "table " -> push caller PID -> call Item::bark -> free temporary string -> implies/pop -> suspend/return (high confidence).

GotHit flow: initialize frame/info bookkeeping -> read two incoming parameters -> test second parameter (bitwise NOT then conditional) -> if condition met call Item::setFrame(frame=1) -> continue return (medium confidence).
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of the numeric info identifier(s) set by the handlers (the 'info' pushed prior to process-exclude) — inferred as bookkeeping but not concretely named (low confidence).
Precise interpretation of the gotHit second parameter and its full set of triggering values beyond the observed sentinel (-1 / 0xFFFF) check (medium confidence).
Whether any other scripts rely on the process-exclude set by these handlers for broader game flow (inferred effect only; medium confidence).

main_function.name: U8P_WTABLE::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to 'U8P_WTABLE::look' (Event 0) by preparing item bookkeeping state and emitting the short description string "table " to the requesting process via Item::bark.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_WTABLE::look) triggered when an actor/player examines the table object; caller PID is supplied as the incoming parameter.

function_map.name: U8P_WTABLE::look
function_map.role: Handle Event 0: set item info/process-exclude and call Item::bark to show 'table ' to the caller.

function_map.name: U8P_WTABLE::gotHit
function_map.role: Handle Event 6: set item info/process-exclude and, when the second parameter matches a sentinel, change the item's frame to 1 (visual change).

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Present/display a short text message coming from the item to a specified process/player.

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Change the visual frame/appearance of the item instance.

external_calls.purpose: Deliver the short U8P_WTABLE::look-description string ("table ") to the requesting process/player identified by caller PID.
Set the item's display frame to 1 when a specific got-hit parameter condition is met (visual/animation change).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_WTABLE::look: When the table object is examined (Event 0) it sets item-level bookkeeping (an info key and process-exclude state), constructs the literal string "table ", and invokes Item::bark to present that string to the requesting process/player. After the bark it performs the VM/handler cleanup (stack/implies/pop) and returns the normal U8P_WTABLE::look-handler result.
